title: pedicularis gruina subsp. pilosa
intro: Pedicularis gruina subsp. pilosa, also known as a species of flowering plant in the bumblebee genus, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and improving digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, Pedicularis gruina subsp. pilosa has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, but its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
